items.html: The matter is that the Yesod has no measure like the other Sefirot; it is smaller than they, and its measure is a quarter of the Tifferet, as stated in parashat Balak, p 204, on the verse "and the number of the fourth part [Rova] of Israel": the Yesod — which brings out their fourths, the drops of seed of ZA [Zeir Anpin], who is called Israel — its measure is a quarter of his Guf [body]. As is written there: "the measure of the Guf is four Britot in its measure, and the Brit [covenant] is a quarter in the measure of the measuring of the Guf," etc. And now she is called Pesia LeVar [a step outward], as above, and she is as great as the measure of the Yesod itself.
